instructions.instruction: Strict separation between Application (Core) and Presentation (View).
instructions.original_snippets: implements a strict separation between the Application (Business Logic, State, Rules) and the Presentation (UI, Inputs, Rendering).
instructions.relevant_when: When designing any new feature or application.

instructions.instruction: The Core must be platform-agnostic and contain zero UI code.
instructions.original_snippets: The 'Core': Holds the 'Source of Truth,' is platform-agnostic, and contains zero UI code.
instructions.relevant_when: When writing code in the Core layer.

instructions.instruction: The Core must compile without UI framework dependencies.
instructions.original_snippets: Constraint: Must compile without any UI framework dependencies.
instructions.relevant_when: When setting up build scripts or importing libraries in the Core layer.

instructions.instruction: The View is a 'Humble Object' that only reflects state.
instructions.original_snippets: The 'View': A 'Humble Object' that strictly reflects the state provided by the Core.
instructions.relevant_when: When writing code in the View layer.
instructions.why_given: Preference - simplifies UI testing and platform porting.

instructions.instruction: Core logic should use Actions -> State -> ViewModel flow.
instructions.original_snippets: Pattern: Receives Actions/Commands -> Mutates State -> Emits Snapshots/ViewModels.
instructions.relevant_when: When designing the State Machine or data flow in the Core.

instructions.instruction: Input must be strongly typed Commands.
instructions.original_snippets: Input: Strongly typed Commands (e.g., SubmitForm { data }).
instructions.relevant_when: When defining the Bridge/Interface for user inputs.
instructions.why_given: Preference - improves type safety across the boundary.

instructions.relevant_when: When defining the Bridge/Interface for data display.
instructions.why_given: Preference - prevents the View from accidentally mutating Core state.

instructions.instruction: Never pass complex internal objects (like DB connections) to the View.
instructions.original_snippets: Rule: Never pass complex internal objects (like DB connections) to the View. Pass simple Data Transfer Objects (DTOs).
instructions.relevant_when: When passing data through the Bridge/Interface.

instructions.instruction: Business-critical validation MUST live in the Core.
instructions.original_snippets: all business-critical validation MUST live in the Core.
instructions.relevant_when: When implementing validation for user inputs.

instructions.instruction: Default to a monorepo structure with specific directories.
instructions.original_snippets: Default to a monorepo structure (e.g., /core for logic, /ui for presentation, and /shared or /bridge for interfaces).
Always define the Core structs, logic, and state first.
instructions.relevant_when: When starting to build a new feature.

instructions.instruction: Explicitly define the FFI boundary or API contract.
instructions.original_snippets: Explicitly define the FFI boundary, WASM bindings, or API contracts between Core and View.
instructions.why_given: New knowledge - technical necessity for cross-platform interop.

instructions.original_snippets: Write the Frontend code assuming the Core library already exists and works perfectly.

instructions.instruction: Refuse business logic in UI components.
instructions.original_snippets: If the user asks to put business logic in a UI component... refuse and move it to the Core, exposing a property in the ViewModel instead.
instructions.relevant_when: When the user requests to add logic to a UI component.

instructions.instruction: Core buffers raw data and applies deltas for real-time data.
instructions.original_snippets: Implements 'Codec' logic for real-time data (buffering, jitter compensation) before exposing clean state to the View.
instructions.relevant_when: When handling real-time data streams like stock prices or sensor data.
instructions.why_given: Preference - handles concurrency and jitter in Core.
